ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Boti Kebab; mapped to the closest parent class 'kebab food product' (FOODON_03302051). Boti Kebab is a boneless cubed lamb kebab grilled in a tandoor with Indian spices.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (lamb meat, grilled) is the closest defensible match. No specific Boti Kebab entry exists in FoodEx2; the preparation method (tandoor grilling) and primary ingredient (boneless lamb) align with this code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Boti Kebab or equivalent lamb kebab preparation.

main_ingredients: Indian spices (cumin, coriander, black pepper, turmeric, chilli)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Boti Kebab in a Singapore restaurant or hawker setting is approximately 150 g, comprising 3 to 4 cubes of boneless grilled lamb.

nutrient_source.title: Boti Kebab — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Boti Kebab (cr_id G00010248). All per-100g values including energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, and cholesterol are taken from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for lamb, composite cuts, cooked (NDB 17283) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Boti Kebab. GI is estimated at approximately 30 (low) based on analogous high-protein, low-carbohydrate grilled meat dishes. Grilled lamb and similar marinated meat kebabs with minimal carbohydrate content (5.24g/100g, primarily from spice marinade) are classified as low-GI foods. The University of Sydney GI Database classifies high-protein, low-carbohydrate meat dishes in the GI range of 0–40. Glycemic load per 150g serving = (30 × 5.24 × 1.5) / 100 = 2.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080 mg per 100 g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Boti Kebab due to its very high sodium content of 1080 mg/100 g; if consumed, request a reduced-salt marinade, limit the portion to no more than 50–75 g, and avoid additional sauces or chutneys.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.24 g/100 g) and low GI (~30) support glycaemic stability, but high fat content (26.4 g/100 g) may slow gastric emptying and delay post-meal glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Boti Kebab is acceptable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes in moderate portions due to its low carbohydrate content and low glycaemic index; however, the high fat content (26.4 g/100 g) may delay gastric emptying and alter post-meal glucose kinetics, so post-meal glucose monitoring is advised, and sweet chutneys or dipping sauces should be avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (26.4 g/100 g), estimated saturated fat (~7.5 g/100 g), high sodium (1080 mg/100 g), and cholesterol (85 mg/100 g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Boti Kebab only occasionally and in small portions (≤75 g), as the combination of high saturated fat (~7.5 g/100 g), high sodium (1080 mg/100 g), and cholesterol (85 mg/100 g) is unfavourable for lipid profiles and blood pressure management; grilled preparation is preferable to fried alternatives, and accompaniment with high-fibre vegetables is recommended.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Lamb is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6) which amplifies RA inflammatory pathways; high sodium (1080 mg/100 g) worsens tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or minimise Boti Kebab as lamb is a primary dietary source of arachidonic acid that directly amplifies TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways; the high sodium content further exacerbates RA-related tissue swelling, and substitution with chicken or fish kebab is recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Boti Kebab is a traditional South Asian grilled meat dish consisting of boneless cubed lamb marinated in a spiced yogurt mixture and cooked in a tandoor (clay oven) at high heat. The name 'boti' refers to small boneless cubes of meat, and the dish is a staple of Indian and Pakistani cuisine, widely served in Singapore's Indian restaurants and hawker centres. The marinade typically includes yogurt, ginger, garlic, cumin, coriander, turmeric, black pepper, red chilli, and salt, which tenderise the lamb and impart a smoky, aromatic flavour. The tandoor cooking method produces a characteristic charred exterior with a juicy, tender interior. Nutritionally, Boti Kebab is energy-dense at 302 kcal per 100 g, with high fat content (26.4 g/100 g, estimated saturated fat ~7.5 g/100 g), moderate protein (10.7 g/100 g), and low carbohydrate (5.24 g/100 g). Sodium is very high at 1080 mg/100 g due to the salt-heavy marinade, making it unsuitable for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease without significant portion restriction. The low carbohydrate content and low glycaemic index (~30) make it relatively acceptable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes in small portions, though the high fat content may delay gastric emptying. Lamb's arachidonic acid content makes this dish a concern for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis. Boti Kebab is typically served as a starter or main course at lunch or dinner, often accompanied by mint chutney, sliced onions, and lemon wedges.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Boti Kebab (cr_id G00010248) with a confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for cooked lamb. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database classification framework for high-protein, low-carbohydrate grilled meat dishes.
notes: 1. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as reported by HPB for this preparation; this is plausible given the minimal vegetable content in the marinade and the primary lamb meat base — confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database. 2. saturated_fat_g (~7.5 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for lamb composite cuts, cooked (NDB 17283), as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific Boti Kebab preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 4. GI is an analogous estimate (~30, low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database classification for high-protein, low-carbohydrate grilled meat dishes; no direct GI measurement for Boti Kebab exists in the published literature. 5. Halal status is assumed based on the dish's Indian Muslim culinary tradition; individual restaurant certification should be verified. 6. Chinese name 博提烤肉串 is a descriptive transliteration/translation; no standard Chinese name exists for this dish in Singapore.
